channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Funeral for slain Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah set for Feb. 23
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Lebanese Hezbollah group Sunday announced the funeral of its longtime leader will take place on Feb. 23, months after he was assassinated in a series of Israeli airstrikes in a southern Beirut suburb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trudeau, Sheinbaum vow to 'enhance the strong bilateral relations' amid US tariffs
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum pledged to “enhance the strong bilateral relations” between their two countries the same day President Trump enacted tariffs on them, according to a readout on Trudeau’s website.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Canadian ambassador to U.S.: Canadians 'perplexed' and 'confused' by Trump's tariffs
item.description: Canada’s ambassador to the U.S., Kirsten Hillman, said on Sunday that Canadians are “perplexed” and “confused” by tariffs enacted by President Trump. “Canadians are perplexed, I think disappointed,” Hillman told ABC News’s George Stephanopoulos.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: UK's Starmer seeks strong trade relations with the US in the wake of Trump's tariffs
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday that he would seek a strong trade relationship with the U.S. after President Donald Trump suggested he would slap Europe with tariffs after he hit America's biggest trading partners — Canada, Mexico and China — with import taxes.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's trade war among allies triggers retaliation from Canada and Mexico
item.description: Canada and Mexico ordered retaliatory tariffs on American goods in response to sweeping tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies And Maria Verza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: France braces for a political showdown as the new prime minister seeks to force a budget through
item.description: Political uncertainty is again looming in France after Prime Minister Francois Bayrou warned that he would use special executive powers this week to get his budget approved without a vote by lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia and Ukraine trade blame over an attack on a boarding school in Russia’s Kursk region
item.description: Competing claims have emerged over a deadly attack on a boarding school in Sudzha, a city in Russia’s Kursk region that has been under Ukrainian control for five months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rubio visiting Central America with the Panama Canal and immigration top of mind
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on his first foreign trip in office, arriving in Central America on Saturday to press President Donald Trump's top priority — curbing illegal immigration — and to bring the message that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: King Charles says 'our hearts' are with United States after 'devastating loss of life' in DC plane crash
item.description: King Charles III said Saturday he was "shocked and saddened" about the deadly midair collision near Reagan National Airport Wednesday, saying his thoughts were with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza ceasefire sees its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The ceasefire in Gaza saw its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners on Saturday, and the crucial Rafah border crossing reopened two days before discussions on the truce’s far more difficult second phase begin.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and IMAD ISSEID, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Hamas frees 3 hostages, Israel releases Palestinians as part of ceasefire deal
item.description: Hamas freed three hostages in the southern Gaza Strip on Saturday and Israel released 183 Palestinian prisoners in the fourth round of exchanges during the Gaza ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arab nations reject Trump’s suggestion to relocate Palestinians from Gaza to Egypt and Jordan
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Powerful Arab nations on Saturday rejected U.S. President Donald Trump’s suggestion to relocate Palestinians from Gaza to neighboring Egypt and Jordan.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Venezuela agreed to accept migrants back, lauds return of hostages
item.description: President Trump said that Venezuela has agreed to accept the country’s migrants living in the U.S. back as he lauded the return of six American hostages who were held in the Latin American country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Russian drone and missile attacks kill 8 in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — At least eight people died overnight as Russian drone and missile strikes pounded Ukraine’s towns and cities, local officials said Saturday. Meanwhile, Moscow’s troops continued their grinding advance through the country’s east.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American among latest hostage release in Gaza as Israel preps prisoner exchange
item.description: An Israeli American was among the latest batch of hostages released from Gaza during the temporary ceasefire between Israel and Palestinian militant group Hamas, according to officials. Hamas freed three hostages on Saturday, including Keith Siegel, 65, an American from North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Dutch police arrest three suspects after the theft of a priceless golden helmet from Romania
item.description: Three suspects have been arrested following the theft of an ancient golden helmet from Romania that had been on display in a museum in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Taliban take over Afghanistan’s only luxury hotel, more than a decade after attacking it
item.description: The Taliban are taking over the operations of Afghanistan’s only luxury hotel in Kabul, more than a decade after they launched a deadly attack there that killed nine people. The Serena Hotel said Friday it was closing its operations in the Afghan capital on Feb.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's foreign aid freeze guts grassroots groups helping migrants in Latin America
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — A busy shelter for migrants in southern Mexico has been left without a doctor. A program to provide mental health support for LGBTQ+ youth fleeing Venezuela was disbanded.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, MARIA VERZA and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey dismisses 5 military academy graduates for taking discontinued secular oath
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Five military academy graduates and three of their immediate superiors were dismissed from the Turkish Armed Forces for taking a pro-secular oath during their graduation ceremony, the defense ministry announced on Friday The Ministry of National Defense launched a disciplinary
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Growing anger in Greece over deadly rail disaster prompts plan by opposition to challenge government
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Three Greek opposition parties vowed Friday to challenge the country’s center-right government with a censure motion over its handling of a deadly rail disaster nearly two years ago.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tens of thousands of Muslims attend annual Biswa Ijtema event in Bangladesh
item.description: TONGI, Bangladesh (AP) — Tens of thousands of people gathered on a riverbank near Bangladesh’s capital on Friday to listen to sermons by Islamic scholars at the Biswa Ijtema, or global congregation of Muslim devotees.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israelis and Palestinians rejoice after more hostages and prisoners are freed
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Eight more hostages were freed from the Gaza Strip by Hamas-led militants on Thursday in a sometimes chaotic process that briefly delayed Israel's release of 110 Palestinian prisoners and underscored the fragility of the ceasefire that began earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, WAFAA SHURAFA and ISABEL DeBRE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: An Iraqi man who carried out Quran burnings in Sweden is killed in a shooting
item.description: Salwan Momika, a 38-year-old Iraqi man who staged several burnings and desecrations of the Quran in Sweden in 2023, has been killed in a shooting near Stockholm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Caribbean nation of St Kitts launches investigation after 19 people found dead in drifting vessel
item.description: Authorities in the Caribbean nation of St. Kitts and Nevis have launched an investigation after discovering a partially submerged boat containing at least 19 bodies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moment IDF soldier is reunited with family seen on video
item.description: Agam Berger, an IDF soldier who was taken hostage during Hamas' attacks on Oct. 7, 2023, is reunited with her family after spending 482 days in captivity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Backlash may shatter President Macron's plans to leave mark on Notre Dame windows
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron’s plans for installing contemporary windows inside the restored Notre Dame Cathedral is facing furious opposition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Hamas hostage details horrors of captivity, credits kidnapped IDF soldier with saving her life
item.description: Former hostage Amit Soussana, who was the first Israeli woman to speak about being sexual assaulted while in Hamas captivity, says kidnapped IDF soldier Liri Albag saved her life.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 30 dead in stampede at the massive Maha Kumbh festival in India, police say
item.description: At least 30 people were killed and another 60 injured in a stampede at the Maha Kumbh festival, a six-week Hindu festival and the world’s largest religious gathering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, WGN 9

item.title: White House: Colombia to take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth over immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean plane crash report says bird remains were found in engines, but no cause yet revealed
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The first report on last month’s Jeju Air crash in South Korea confirmed traces of bird strikes in the plane's engines, though officials haven’t determined the cause of the accident that killed all but two of the 181 people on board.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation
